transcript.final_response:
The deep merge completed successfully. The result has been saved to `/config/merged.json`.

## Result

```json
{
  "app": {
    "debug": false,
    "name": "myservice",
    "port": 8080
  },
  "db": {
    "host": "db.prod.internal",
    "pool_size": 20,
    "port": 5432
  },
  "log": {
    "format": "json",
    "level": "warn"
  }
}
```

### What happened during the merge

The deep merge recursed into each nested object rather than replacing it wholesale, so values were combined correctly:

| Key | Source | Reason |
|-----|--------|--------|
| `app.name` = `"myservice"` | defaults | only present in defaults — **preserved** |
| `app.port` = `8080` | production | overrode default `3000` |
| `app.debug` = `false` | production | overrode default `true` |
| `db.host` = `"db.prod.internal"` | production | overrode default `localhost` |
| `db.pool_size` = `20` | production | overrode default `5` |
| `db.port` = `5432` | defaults | only present in defaults — **preserved** |
| `log.level` = `"warn"` | production | overrode default `debug` |
| `log.format` = `"json"` | production | overrode default `text` |

The key behavior to note: `db.port` survived even though production also defined other `db` keys. A shallow merge would have lost it by replacing the whole `db` object — the recursive `deepmerge` function preserved it.

A note on what I did: `node` wasn't available, and my first `jq -s` attempt produced `null` values because of a scoping quirk in how `.[0]`/`.[1]` interacted with the `reduce` body. I switched to passing the two files in via `--slurpfile` arguments (`$def[0]`, `$prod[0]`), which resolved it cleanly.
tool_calls: cat /config/defaults.json; echo "---"; cat /config/production.json; echo "---"; which jq node

jq -s '
def deepmerge(a; b):
  reduce (b | keys_unsorted[]) as $k
    (a;
      if (a[$k] | type) == "object" and (b[$k] | type) == "object"
      then .[$k] = deepmerge(a[$k]; b[$k])
      else .[$k] = b[$k]
      end);
deepmerge(.[0]; .[1])
' /config/defaults.json /config/production.json > /config/merged.json

echo "=== merged.json ==="
cat /config/merged.json

jq --version

# Test the deepmerge function in isolation
jq -n '
def deepmerge(a; b):
  reduce (b | keys_unsorted[]) as $k
    (a;
      if (a[$k] | type) == "object" and (b[$k] | type) == "object"
      then .[$k] = deepmerge(a[$k]; b[$k])
      else .[$k] = b[$k]
      end);
deepmerge({"x":{"a":1,"b":2}}; {"x":{"b":9,"c":3}})
'

jq -n \
  --slurpfile def /config/defaults.json \
  --slurpfile prod /config/production.json '
def deepmerge(a; b):
  reduce (b | keys_unsorted[]) as $k
    (a;
      if (a[$k] | type) == "object" and (b[$k] | type) == "object"
      then .[$k] = deepmerge(a[$k]; b[$k])
      else .[$k] = b[$k]
      end);
deepmerge($def[0]; $prod[0])
' > /config/merged.json

echo "=== merged.json ==="
cat /config/merged.json

echo "=== merged.json ==="
